verdict_reasoning: ATG9A is a multi-pass transmembrane protein (6 TM domains) resident in the endomembrane system — Golgi apparatus, endosomes, and phagophore assembly sites (PAS). It cycles between these compartments during autophagy initiation and has no documented stable surface display mechanism. No evidence of: (1) cell-state-induced surface recruitment — ATG9A activation/cycling is autophagy-flux-dependent, not stress-display-dependent; (2) tissue-restricted surface lineage — expression is ubiquitous and compartment-restricted, not lineage-restricted; (3) lysosomal exocytosis — ATG9A is a Golgi/endosomal protein that traffics via retromer and AP-3 pathways, not by lysosomal fusion; (4) dual PM localization — no proteomics, flow cytometry, or biotinylation data report a PM pool; (5) surface-attached partner — ATG9A acts as a lipid scramblase/transporter within autophagic membrane biogenesis, not as a cargo or partner of a PM-anchored receptor. Phospholipid scramblase activity and PAS localization are consistent with an endomembrane-resident catalytic protein.
